allergies.reaction: Rash (childhood, per family report — non-anaphylactic, never re-challenged)
allergies.notes: Patient was treated with amoxicillin as a child in Vietnam (~1960s) and developed a rash per her mother's account. No anaphylaxis or angioedema. Has not received any penicillin-class antibiotic since immigration. Risk/benefit of re-challenge has been discussed with PCP previously; she preferred to avoid.

allergies.reaction: Urticaria + facial flushing (during 2019 CT for knee evaluation)
allergies.notes: Treated successfully with IV diphenhydramine and observation. Subsequent CT studies have been pre-medicated with prednisone (50 mg PO at 13, 7, and 1 hours pre-procedure) + diphenhydramine 50 mg PO 1 hour pre-procedure per Hopkins Radiology pre-medication protocol. Tolerated this premedication for cholelithiasis US-guided procedures and for 2027-02-15 admission CT A/P (pre-medicated emergently in the ED).

reviewedBy: Emergency Department intake, Dr. K. Patel
